design_brief.goal: Establish Guy's Classy Shoes as a destination for discerning men seeking heirloom-quality leather footwear—positioning the brand as the antithesis of trend-chasing, emphasizing restrained heritage craftsmanship over logo-driven luxury.
design_brief.audience: Affluent, professionally accomplished men aged 35–70 who value heritage, materials, and understated elegance. Men who buy shoes to be taken seriously, not to be seen. Likely wear them to boardrooms, formal events, and occasions where discretion signals confidence.

visual_direction.layout: Spacious and breathing. Let photography dominate. Hero section should occupy full screen. Generous margins and white space throughout. Avoid visual clutter—every element curated. Product grids should showcase one or two hero shots per category before deeper exploration.
visual_direction.typography: Playfair Display or Georgia for headings (serif, classical, authoritative). Inter for body copy and UI (clean, readable, modern restraint). Generous line heights (1.6–1.8). Heading hierarchy should feel editorial, not commercial.
visual_direction.color_usage: Charcoal (#1A1A1A) as primary text and structural framework. Cream (#FAF7F2) as background—lets imagery breathe. Warm gold (#C9A96E) as accent for CTAs, highlighted collections, testimonial quotes. Rich burgundy (#4A1A2C) used sparingly in secondary contexts (dividers, subtle backgrounds) to add depth and luxury feeling. Avoid stacking all colors on one page.
visual_direction.motion: Subtle and refined. Slow fade-ins on scroll. Gentle image transitions. No scrolljacking, parallax, or playful micro-interactions—those feel cheap at this price point. Restraint over spectacle.
visual_direction.density: Spacious. Negative space is a design choice, not an oversight. Content feels curated and intentional. Product pages should emphasize one shoe at a time with room to appreciate detail.

content_priorities: Product imagery and material detail (hero photography must convey quality, hand-stitching, leather grain)
Guy's origin story and craftsmanship philosophy (emotional anchor)
Heritage, sourcing, and materials authenticity (proof of claim)
Social proof via testimonials from discerning customers
Clear path to inquiry, appointment booking, or purchase intent
must_have_sections: Hero with luxury shoe or craftsmanship imagery
Featured collections preview (teaser to Collections page)
Brand story teaser with link to About
Testimonials section (3–4 quotes from satisfied customers)
Collections page with category grid (Classic Oxfords, Exotic Leathers, Loafers, Boots)
About page: Guy's story, craftsmanship philosophy, materials sourcing detail
Contact page: physical location, inquiry form, appointment booking widget
avoid: Pressure sales tactics or urgency messaging ('limited time,' 'only 3 left'—contradicts restrained positioning)
Excessive product listings or catalog sprawl (quality over quantity; curate)
Bright, saturated colors or playful imagery (feels juvenile for $1000+ shoes)
Fast animations, parallax scrolling, or scrolljacking (reads as insecurity, not luxury)
Social media feeds or user-generated content (doesn't fit curated brand voice)
Logo repetition beyond header and footer
Mobile-first layouts that sacrifice desktop elegance (this audience uses desktop)
Discount language or sale promotion (erodes positioning)
Testimonial photos from everyday settings (should feel curated, not casual)
open_questions: Is product photography already shot/available, or will design brief need to guide a photography style and shoot strategy?
Is this a branding/information site with inquiry/appointment booking, or does it need e-commerce functionality (cart, checkout, inventory)? This affects page structure and CTA hierarchy.

tech_recommendations.reason: Dynamic features detected — needs server-side logic for booking
Needs server-side rendering or API routes

design_decisions.decision: Built premium home page following the design brief precisely: full-screen hero, curated collections preview, brand philosophy section, testimonials, and clear CTAs
Applied approved color palette (charcoal #1A1A1A, cream #FAF7F2, gold #C9A96E) via CSS custom properties for brand consistency
Used Playfair Display for headings and Inter for body, with generous line heights and letter spacing to convey editorial sophistication and restraint
Identical nav/footer/typography** across all pages—same sticky nav with logo, same 4-column footer, same Playfair Display + Inter fonts, same CSS custom properties for brand colors.
